subject: Geography
title: Los Angeles: The Sprawl Capital
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Los Angeles: The Sprawl Capital.

hook.text: Los Angeles imports its water from over 500 km away because the city is built in a desert.

buildup.text: The LA Aqueduct, opened in 1913, drained the Owens Valley to feed the growing city.

discovery.text: Freeways replaced streetcars in the 1950s, creating America's most car-dependent city.

twist.text: The Owens Valley became a dust bowl. Residents called the aqueduct theft, not engineering.

climax.text: LA's survival depends on water from distant rivers, snowpacks, and aquifers—all under stress.

punchline.text: A desert city of 13 million that can't survive on its own water.

quiz.question: Where does most of Los Angeles' water come from?
